questions.original_question: 路特斯科技的全年销售指引是多少？目前看是否能完成？
questions.answer: 路特斯科技2024年的全年销售指引为12,000台车。这一目标基于对全球宏观市场环境和关税不确定性的审慎评估。尽管面临贸易不确定性和关税上涨等挑战，公司在2024年上半年的交付量达到了4,873台，同比增长239%，显示出强劲的增长势头。根据2024年8月28日的最新数据，路特斯科技预计全年交付量将达到12,134台，表明公司正在稳步推进其销售目标。因此，路特斯科技有望在2024年实现其全年销售指引。

rag_info.chunk_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

rag_info.chunk_content: "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering pursuit of performance and excellence, the company made steady progress with continuing operational growth in the first half of 2024," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. "In the first half of 2024, the deliveries soared to nearly 4,900 vehicles, up 239% year-on-year. Lotus Technology achieved an even more balanced distribution of deliveries in all key markets globally. The US market recorded extraordinary growth after Lotus Technology's re-entry into the market, contributing to 26% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology launched the 'Win26' plan to increase resilience, strengthen the brand, and strive for sustainable growth. Going forward, Lotus Technology will keep executing its plans, boosting efficiencies and competitiveness, and remain committed to its customers, investors, and stakeholders." "Lotus Technology continued delivery of strong topline growth in the first half of 2024, with total revenues and gross profit up by 206% and 731% year-over-year respectively,” said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer. "Gross margin for the first half of 2024 increased by 8 percentage points year-over-year but shrank 2 percentage points compared to year end of 2023 as a result of increased cost of the Emira sportscar model due to inflation and seasonal variation in product sales mix. Lotus Technology's average selling price (ASP) remained above \$100,000 in the first half of 2024 despite intensified competition. The execution of the 'Win26' plan will enable Lotus Technology to achieve positive EBITDA and operating cash flow in 2026, to boost financial performance, strengthen the balance sheet, and deliver commitments to shareholders.”

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

rag_info.chunk_content: The increase in deliveries was driven primarily by the Eletre, Lotus Technology's first lifestyle BEV, which ramped up production and sales in the second half of the year. Lotus Technology also continued to expand its global presence, laying the foundation for the ongoing global roll-out of the brand's vehicles with an increase of 46 stores year-on-year to a total of 215 locations as of December 31, 2023. Over the past year, Lotus Technology notably opened new flagship stores in prime locations, including in the Golden Triangle area of Paris, Mayfair in London, Gangnam-gu in Seoul, and other strategically important global cities. 2024 will be a pivotal year for Lotus Technology Inc.'s execution of its Vision80 Strategy, as the company further ramps up global deliveries of its leading product portfolio in new markets. In March 2024, the company began deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer. Emeya deliveries are now underway in China and are expected to begin in Europe in the third quarter of 2024. Lotus Technology's Eletre hyper-SUV is also set to enter more markets, including the U.S., over the course of 2024. In 2024, the company expects to increase its total deliveries about threefold to 26,000 and further grow its healthy gross margin to 17-19%. "Lotus Technology is pleased with the early progress and promising results achieved in 2023, with increasing deliveries reflecting the strength of the brand and ramping production," said Lotus Tech CEO Qingfeng Feng.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York – November 12, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced its vehicle delivery results for the first ten months of 2024. Lotus Technology Inc. continues to achieve top-rate growth among traditional luxury car brands, delivering 8,631 vehicles in the first ten months of 2024. Europe and China continued to be the major markets for Lotus Technology, with each region contributing about 35% and 25% of the total deliveries, respectively. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. started an open beta test for its urban NOA (Navigate on Autopilot) in key cities. Certain over-the-air updates of intelligent driving, including highway assistant driving functions, were launched in Europe. As previously announced, Lotus Robotics, Lotus Technology's intelligent driving arm, provides intelligent driving solutions with global coverage to both Lotus-brand intelligent vehicles and other global leading auto conglomerates such as Lynk&Co, Farizon Auto, and a top Japanese Tier-one supplier. In October, Lotus Technology Inc. was awarded the Highly Commends honor at the Reuters Sustainability Awards 2024 in the Business Transformation category as the only awardee from the automotive industry. Note 1: Invoiced deliveries, including commissioned deliveries in the US market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content:
How was Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of the year? Total revenue for the first half of 2024 was $398 million, representing a year-on-year increase of 206%. Lotus Technology's Average Selling Price (ASP) remained above $100,000, achieving growth far exceeding the industry and luxury car segment. Lotus Technology's gross margin for the first half of this year was 13%, compared to 5% in the same period of 2023.

In the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology achieved total product deliveries of 4,873 units, a 239% year-on-year increase, making it the fastest-growing brand among traditional luxury car brands. Global deliveries were balanced, with the four major markets (Europe, China, North America, and Rest of World) each contributing 20-30% of deliveries.

In the first half of 2024, as Lotus Technology began collaborating with several leading automotive brands by providing intelligent driving solutions and technology development services, technology-related revenue started to expand. With the continued increase in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware, the potential for generating paid software subscription revenue continues to grow.

Lotus Technology launched the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service, offering personalized options, designer customization, limited editions, and exclusive customization to meet the growing demands of its customers. The delivery of customized models is expected to further enhance the brand's luxury positioning and benefit Lotus Technology's long-term profit margins.

This surge in revenue was accompanied by an improvement in gross margin, which rose to 13%, up from 5% during the same period in 2023.

Deliveries were well-distributed across major markets, with Europe, China, North America, and the Rest of World each contributing between 20-30% to the total.

The growth in cumulative deliveries of lifestyle vehicles equipped with advanced hardware is anticipated to create additional opportunities for paid software subscription revenue.

Furthermore, the introduction of the Colin Chapman Bespoke Service has allowed Lotus to offer personalized options and designer customizations, catering to evolving customer demands. This initiative is expected to strengthen the brand's luxury positioning and positively influence long-term profit margins.

Overall, Lotus Technology's performance in the first half of 2024 reflects strong financial growth, strategic market positioning, and a commitment to enhancing customer experience, setting a solid foundation for continued success in the luxury automotive sector.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology recorded revenue of \$679 million in 2023, driven by growing sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company achieved a gross profit margin of 15% in the first year of BEV deliveries, with the scaling of asset-light production. Lotus Technology expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, with new flagship locations in Paris, London, and Seoul. The company expects to achieve about a threefold increase in deliveries in 2024 amid steady market expansion. New York - April 8, 2024 - Lotus Technology Inc. ("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. The results reflect steady progress in Lotus Technology's execution of its Vision80 strategy to become an all-electric, intelligent, and luxury mobility provider before Lotus Technology's 80th anniversary in 2028. In its first year of battery electric vehicle (BEV) delivery, Lotus Technology achieved revenue of \$679 million and a gross profit margin of 15% in 2023. Revenue in the fourth quarter of 2023 was \$361 million, representing a 92% quarter-on-quarter increase. This strong performance was enabled by Lotus Technology's asset-light business model, as the company ramped up production and sales of BEV models. Lotus Technology delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, an annual record in the 76-year history of the Lotus brand, with battery electric vehicle (BEV) models representing 63% of total deliveries. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

title: Lotus Technology Reports Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results 
summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. The company recorded a gross profit margin of 15% in its inaugural year of BEV deliveries, benefiting from an asset-light production model. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: "Through Lotus Technology's unwavering pursuit of performance and excellence, the company made steady progress with continuing operational growth in the first half of 2024," said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer. Lotus Technology achieved an even more balanced distribution of deliveries in all key markets globally. The US market recorded extraordinary growth after Lotus Technology’s re-entry into the market, contributing to 26% of total deliveries. Lotus Technology launched the 'Win26' plan to increase resilience, strengthen the brand, and strive for sustainable growth. Going forward, Lotus Technology will keep executing its plans, boosting efficiencies and competitiveness, and remain committed to its customers, investors, and stakeholders." "Lotus Technology continued delivery of strong topline growth in the first half of 2024, with total revenues and gross profit up by 206% and 731% year-over-year respectively,” said Mr. Alexious Lee, Chief Financial Officer. "Gross margin for the first half of 2024 increased by 8 percentage points year-over-year but shrank 2 percentage points compared to year end of 2023 as a result of increased cost of the Emira sportscar model due to inflation and seasonal variation in product sales mix. Lotus Technology's average selling price (ASP) remained above \$100,000 in the first half of 2024 despite intensified competition. The execution of the 'Win26' plan will enable Lotus Technology to achieve positive EBITDA and operating cash flow in 2026, to boost financial performance, strengthen the balance sheet, and deliver commitments to shareholders.”

In 2024, the company plans to triple its delivery volume and strengthen client relationships by introducing four new delivery models. Key initiatives will include optimizing operational efficiency, reducing costs, and implementing best practices to streamline operations. Additionally, targeted marketing, improved customer service, and a refined product assortment will be prioritized to maximize revenue per location, with continuous assessment and adaptation being crucial to achieving these goals.

Performance metrics from 2018 to 2024 indicate a remarkable growth trajectory for Lotus Cars. Sales volume increased from an average of 1,200 units annually (2018-2022) to 6,970 units in 2023, with projections for 2024 reaching 26,000 units. Revenue followed a similar trend, jumping from an average of $100 million during 2018-2022 to $679 million in 2023, with estimates for 2024 ranging between $2,500 million and $2,700 million. The gross profit margin also improved significantly, rising from an average of 3% (2018-2022) to 15% in 2023, with projections for 2024 suggesting further growth to between 17% and 19%. 

Overall, these metrics highlight Lotus Cars' ambitious trajectory towards becoming a leader in the luxury electric vehicle market, with a strong focus on growth in production, revenue, and profitability.

title: 
summary: **Summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s Financial Performance and Strategic Growth (Q1 2024)**

In the first quarter of 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrated remarkable growth across key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. The company reported revenues of $173 million, reflecting an impressive year-over-year increase of 811%, alongside deliveries of approximately 2,194 vehicles, a 731% rise from 264 units in Q1 2023. The gross profit margin for the quarter was 18%, a significant improvement from just 1% a year prior, although it slightly decreased from the previous quarter's 19%. Despite these gains, the company faced a net loss of $258 million, an increase from a $160 million loss in Q1 2023.

Lotus's strategic initiatives include expanding its global presence, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025. The availability of its vehicle models is also set to improve, with the Emira expected to achieve full availability in stores by 2024, while the Eletre and Emeya will follow suit in subsequent years.

Financially, the company has seen substantial growth since 2018, with sales volume skyrocketing from an average of 1,200 units annually to an estimated 26,000 units in 2024. Revenue projections for 2024 are between $2.5 billion and $2.7 billion, up from $679 million in 2023. The gross profit margin is expected to further improve to between 17% and 19%.

As of March 31, 2024, total assets rose to $2.54 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. The company continues to navigate challenges, including net losses and rising operational costs, while focusing on enhancing shareholder value and operational efficiency.

title: Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

In the first nine months of 2024, the Company achieved significant growth, delivering a total of 7,617 units, representing a remarkable 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Deliveries were well-distributed across various regions, with each contributing between 18% to 35% to the overall total, supported by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally. The European market stood out with an exceptional 372% YoY increase in deliveries, while the Rest of the World experienced a 110% growth. Notably, new deliveries commenced for the Emira model in South Africa and India, and the Eletre model in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. Additionally, the unveiling of the Eletre Carbon variant for North America is anticipated, with deliveries expected to start in the first half of 2025.

Sales figures for the period included 3,983 units of "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan," reflecting a 126% increase from 1,762 units in 2023, and 3,634 units of "Sportscars," which marked a 149% rise from 1,459 units in the previous year. Regionally, Europe accounted for 2,683 units (35% share) in 2024, a significant increase from 568 units (18% share) in 2023. China recorded 1,880 units (25% share), a slight decline from 1,993 units (62% share) in 2023. North America reported 1,665 units (22% share) with no prior data for comparison, while the Rest of the World achieved 1,389 units (18% share), up from 660 units (20% share) in 2023.

Financially, the Company reported revenue of 19 units for the first nine months of 2024, representing a substantial 217% YoY increase from 6 units in 2023. Revenue from Lotus increased to 8 units, reflecting a 100% rise, while revenue from other customers surged to 11 units, a remarkable 450% increase from 2 units in 2023. Furthermore, Lotus Robotics secured contracts valued at approximately $130 million for intelligent driving solutions with various automakers, significantly enhancing its technological footprint within the industry.

Overall, the first nine months of 2024 demonstrated the Company's robust growth trajectory, marked by increased deliveries, expanded market presence, and substantial revenue growth, positioning it favorably for future developments.

title: Operational Benchmarking 
summary: The LCAA Board conducted a comprehensive analysis of Lotus Tech's financial performance, focusing on key metrics such as estimated revenue growth rate, gross margin, and EBITDA margin in comparison to its peers, including Tesla, Lucid, Polestar, NIO, Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin. This evaluation utilized Wall Street analyst consensus forecasts for the years 2023 to 2025, based on publicly available data as of January 30, 2023.

The analysis revealed that Lotus Tech is projected to achieve a remarkable revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 90.0%. However, it faces challenges with gross margins estimated between -18.0% and -23.0%, while its EBITDA margin for 2025 is expected to exceed 5.0%. In contrast, Tesla is anticipated to have a revenue CAGR of 28.4% with improving gross margins and a robust EBITDA margin of 21% in 2025. Lucid is expected to lead with the highest revenue CAGR at 125.7%, but it will struggle with negative gross margins initially. Other competitors like Polestar, NIO, Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin show varying growth rates and profitability metrics, with Ferrari and Porsche exhibiting strong gross margins and EBITDA margins.

Additionally, the Board reviewed the estimated enterprise value-to-sales (EV/Sales) multiples for Lotus Tech against its peers for 2024 and 2025. Lotus Tech's EV/Sales multiple is projected to be approximately 0.9x in 2024, declining to around 0.6x in 2025. In comparison, Tesla's multiples are significantly higher, starting at 7.2x in 2024 and decreasing to 5.6x in 2025. Ferrari leads the group with the highest multiples, estimated at 8.4x for 2024 and 7.7x for 2025, while other brands like Polestar, NIO, and Aston Martin exhibit lower multiples.

Overall, the analysis provides a detailed overview of Lotus Tech's ambitious growth trajectory alongside its established competitors, highlighting both the challenges and opportunities within the luxury sports car market. The findings are summarized in accompanying tables that outline operational metrics and enterprise value-to-sales multiples, reflecting market perceptions of growth and profitability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Financial Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Financial Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

For the first nine months of 2024, the company achieved total revenues of $653 million, reflecting a remarkable 105% increase compared to $318 million during the same period in 2023. This growth was primarily driven by sales of goods, which totaled $624 million, marking a 104% year-over-year increase. Additionally, service revenues reached $29 million, representing a significant 129% increase from the previous year.

Despite the impressive revenue growth, the company experienced a decline in gross margin, which fell to 9% from 11% in 2023. The gross margin for goods sold specifically decreased to 7%, down from 10% in the prior year, largely due to destocking activities. In contrast, the gross margin for service revenues improved significantly to 55%, up from 28% in 2023, attributed to a rise in high-margin technology-related income.

The operating loss for the first nine months of 2024 was $598 million, an 18% increase from the $508 million loss reported in the same period of 2023. The net loss for the period also increased by 27%, totaling $667 million compared to $527 million in the previous year. When excluding share-based compensation expenses, the adjusted net loss (non-GAAP) was $633 million, reflecting a 20% year-over-year increase. Furthermore, the adjusted EBITDA (non-GAAP) indicated a loss of $563 million, which is a 16% increase in losses compared to the $486 million loss recorded in 2023.

In summary, while the company demonstrated substantial revenue growth in the first nine months of 2024, it concurrently faced escalating operating and net losses, alongside a decline in gross margins for goods sold.

("Lotus Tech" or "the Company"), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced its unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and the year ended December 31, 2023. Vehicle deliveries in the fourth quarter of 2023 increased nearly 110% quarter-over-quarter to 3,749.

summary: Lotus Technology Inc. has demonstrated significant growth and a strong commitment to expanding its global presence, as evidenced by its financial performance and strategic initiatives. The company reported an impressive 811% increase in revenue year-over-year for the first quarter of 2024, reaching $173 million, driven by a substantial rise in vehicle deliveries. This growth trajectory is supported by a shift towards higher-margin operations and an asset-light business model.

The brand's legacy in motorsports is highlighted by its historical achievements, including multiple championships and prestigious race wins, reinforcing its reputation in the automotive industry. Lotus is also focused on expanding its sales network, with plans to increase points of sale from 215 in 2023 to over 300 by 2025, alongside enhancing vehicle availability for models like the Emira and Eletre.

The company’s balance sheet shows a notable rise in total assets, alongside increased liabilities, indicating ongoing investments and operational costs.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is navigating a complex landscape of rapid growth and financial challenges, while maintaining its legacy in automotive excellence and motorsports. The company's strategic focus on expanding its market presence and improving operational efficiency positions it for potential future success, despite current financial hurdles.

summary: The financial overview of Lotus Technology Inc. highlights significant growth and challenges across various metrics for the periods ending June 30, 2024, compared to the same periods in 2023. The bar chart analysis indicates a remarkable increase in financial figures, with quarterly revenues rising from **$130 million** in 1H23 to **$398 million** in 1H24, and from **$110 million** in 2Q23 to **$225 million** in 2Q24. Deliveries also surged, with 4,873 units delivered in 1H24, marking a **239%** year-over-year increase.

Despite these positive trends in revenue and deliveries, the company faced escalating costs, leading to increased operating losses and net losses. The operating loss expanded to **$438 million** in 1H24, and the net loss reached **$460 million**, reflecting ongoing investments and operational challenges. The adjusted EBITDA also showed a decline, indicating that while revenues grew, the company is still navigating significant financial hurdles.

In terms of assets and liabilities, total assets increased from **$1.58 billion** to **$2.51 billion**, driven by growth in current assets. However, total liabilities also rose, resulting in a narrower shareholders' deficit, which improved from **$(571 million)** to **$(202 million)**.

Additionally, the company is focused on strategic growth, as evidenced by its ambitious production targets, aiming for over **30,000 units** by 2026, and a gross profit margin exceeding **20%**. The vehicle lineup includes exclusive models like the Emeya x Blossom Enchantment and the Evija x Fittipaldi, emphasizing Lotus' commitment to luxury and performance.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates robust growth potential and a strong market presence, it continues to face challenges related to rising costs and losses, necessitating careful management of its financial strategies moving forward.

- **Table Summary:** This visual showcases the key milestones and achievements of Lotus Technology Inc. across its history, from 1957 to 2023, highlighting specific innovations, victories, and recognitions in motorsport and luxury design. It also provides a summary of Lotus Technology Inc.'s accolades, including championship wins, Grand Prix victories, and prestigious awards.

[Row Level]
- **Row 1:** In 1957, the Lotus Eleven achieved its "First Le Mans Win" in the 750cc Index Class, cementing Lotus Technology Inc.'s presence in endurance racing.
- **Row 2:** In 1960, the Lotus Type 18 secured its "First Grand Prix Win" at Monte Carlo, marking Lotus Technology Inc.'s success in Formula 1 racing.
- **Row 3:** In 1963, the Lotus Type 25 achieved a double milestone by winning the "First Formula 1 Constructors' Championship" as well as the "First Formula 1 Drivers' Championship," showcasing the excellence of the car and team.
- **Row 4:** In 1965, the Lotus Type 38 claimed "First INDY 500 Victory," becoming the first rear-engine car to win this prestigious event, revolutionizing the race landscape.
- **Row 5:** In 1978, the Lotus Sunbeam won the "First World Rally Constructors' Championship," demonstrating Lotus Technology Inc.'s versatility in both on-road and rally formats.
- **Row 6:** In 2019, Lotus Technology Inc. was recognized as the "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the prestigious Luxury Briefing Awards, celebrating its influence in the premium automotive industry.
- **Row 7:** In 2020, the Lotus Evija secured the "2020 MUSE Global Design Awards," highlighting its groundbreaking innovation in design.
- **Row 8:** In 2023, the Lotus Eletre was named the "2023 GQ SUV of the Year," reflecting its impact in the SUV market as a luxury electric vehicle.
- **Row 9:** In 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. earned the "Carwow Brand Listing of Lotus," establishing its brand impact and visibility for that year.

**Achievements Summary (Laurel Section):**
- Lotus Technology Inc. has won **7 FIA Formula 1 Constructors' World Championships** and **6 FIA Formula 1 Drivers' World Championships**, demonstrating dominance in F1 racing.
- Lotus Technology Inc. boasts an impressive **81 FIA Formula 1 Grand Prix Wins**, along with **9 Le Mans Wins** in class, testament to its endurance racing legacy.
- Lotus Technology Inc. has achieved **1 Indianapolis 500 Grand Prix Win** and **1 FIA World Rally Constructors' Championship**, highlighting its innovations across multiple motorsport disciplines.

summary: In 2023, Lotus Tech achieved significant milestones, reporting a revenue of $679 million, driven by strong sales of lifestyle battery electric vehicles (BEVs) and sports cars. Lotus Tech expanded its global footprint to 215 stores, including new flagship locations in major cities like Paris, London, and Seoul, and delivered a record 6,970 vehicles, with BEVs making up 63% of total deliveries. The fourth quarter alone saw a remarkable 92% increase in revenue, reaching $361 million, and nearly 110% growth in vehicle deliveries compared to the previous quarter, largely due to the success of the Eletre lifestyle BEV.

Looking forward, Lotus Tech anticipates a nearly threefold increase in deliveries in 2024, targeting a total of 26,000 units while aiming to enhance its gross margin to between 17-19%. The company is set to expand its Vision80 strategy, which seeks to transform Lotus into a fully electric and luxury mobility provider by 2028. Deliveries of the Emeya, its first electric hyper-grand tourer, have begun in China, with European deliveries expected in the third quarter. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed optimism about the company's progress, emphasizing a commitment to long-term growth and value creation for customers and investors as they leverage their luxury brand and advanced technology.

Current liabilities remained relatively stable, while total liabilities increased to $2.52 billion. Notably, shareholders' equity turned positive, reaching $19.8 million, a significant recovery from a deficit of $1.21 billion at the end of 2023.

Overall, Lotus Technology Inc. is on a robust growth trajectory, marked by significant increases in sales, revenue, and profitability, as well as strategic plans for market expansion and product availability. summary: The Company is seeking shareholder approval to amend its memorandum and articles of association, focusing on the redemption rights of Class A ordinary shareholders in connection with an initial Business Combination. The proposed amendments aim to modify the conditions under which shares can be redeemed, particularly if the Business Combination is not completed by the extended liquidation date. Public Shareholders who redeem their shares will not be eligible for funds from the Trust Account upon completion of a Business Combination or liquidation if the Company fails to finalize the transaction within the designated timeframe.

Financial contributions from the Sponsor will support these extensions, with provisions for additional funding if necessary. Shareholders have the option to redeem their shares for cash based on the Trust Account's total amount, which currently stands at approximately $10.00 per Public Share.

During an Extraordinary General Meeting, a significant number of Class A ordinary shares were redeemed, and the Company has since approved several extensions of its Termination Date, with the latest extending to March 15, 2024. If the Company fails to complete the Business Combination by this date, it will cease operations and redeem Public Shares, adhering to legal requirements.

The Company has also made adjustments to its warrants, which are classified as liabilities and measured at fair value. The valuation of these warrants has been based on market prices and Monte Carlo simulations, with ongoing assessments reflecting changes in fair value. The financial statements include reconciliations of ordinary shares and net income per share, highlighting the performance of Class A and Class B shares over the past two years.

title: Operational Benchmarking 
summary: The LCAA Board conducted a comprehensive analysis of Lotus Tech's financial performance, focusing on key metrics such as estimated revenue growth rate, gross margin, and EBITDA margin in comparison to its peers, including Tesla, Lucid, Polestar, NIO, Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin. This evaluation utilized Wall Street analyst consensus forecasts for the years 2023 to 2025, based on publicly available data as of January 30, 2023.

The analysis revealed that Lotus Tech is projected to achieve a remarkable revenue compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 90.0%. However, it faces challenges with gross margins estimated between -18.0% and -23.0%, while its EBITDA margin for 2025 is expected to exceed 5.0%. In contrast, Tesla is anticipated to have a revenue CAGR of 28.4% with improving gross margins and a robust EBITDA margin of 21% in 2025. Lucid is expected to lead with the highest revenue CAGR at 125.7%, but it will struggle with negative gross margins initially. Other competitors like Polestar, NIO, Ferrari, Porsche, and Aston Martin show varying growth rates and profitability metrics, with Ferrari and Porsche exhibiting strong gross margins and EBITDA margins.

Additionally, the Board reviewed the estimated enterprise value-to-sales (EV/Sales) multiples for Lotus Tech against its peers for 2024 and 2025. Lotus Tech's EV/Sales multiple is projected to be approximately 0.9x in 2024, declining to around 0.6x in 2025. In comparison, Tesla's multiples are significantly higher, starting at 7.2x in 2024 and decreasing to 5.6x in 2025. Ferrari leads the group with the highest multiples, estimated at 8.4x for 2024 and 7.7x for 2025, while other brands like Polestar, NIO, and Aston Martin exhibit lower multiples.

Overall, the analysis provides a detailed overview of Lotus Tech's ambitious growth trajectory alongside its established competitors, highlighting both the challenges and opportunities within the luxury sports car market. The findings are summarized in accompanying tables that outline operational metrics and enterprise value-to-sales multiples, reflecting market perceptions of growth and profitability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: By building upon Lotus Technology's iconic sports car reputation, along with the ambition to invest in a lifestyle business, the company has established a strong commercial organizational competence. Lotus Technology believes that one of the most effective forms of marketing is to continually improve customers’ experience. The omni-channel sales model is customer-oriented, allowing customers to access the online platform to customize their vehicles, make payments, and order aftersales services, as well as visit in person, consult the sales team, and reserve test-driving sessions. In December 2022, Lotus Technology hosted the Eletre Hyper Track test drive event at the Shanghai International Circuit, where the Lotus Eletre R+ (an enhanced version of the Eletre R in China) made its first media test drive on a Formula One track. In September 2023, Lotus Technology debuted the Emeya, its first electric hyperGT, in New York. The company continues to build a consumer community and enhance brand awareness globally. On March 29, 2023, and 2024, Lotus Technology hosted Lotus Day events on the racetrack to engage customers with the inspiration of driving passion and racing culture, in conjunction with the delivery ceremony of the Eletre and Emeya.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Branding and Marketing 
summary: The company strategically leverages its iconic reputation in the sportscar market while simultaneously investing in a lifestyle business to enhance its commercial capabilities. A cornerstone of its marketing strategy is the improvement of customer experience through an omni-channel sales model. This model allows customers to customize vehicles online, make payments, and access aftersales services, while also providing opportunities to visit physical locations for consultations and test drives.

Significant milestones in the company's marketing efforts include the Eletre Hyper Track test drive at the Shanghai International Circuit in December 2022, which showcased the Lotus Eletre R+. Additionally, the debut of the Emeya, the first electric hyperGT, took place in New York in September 2023, marking a pivotal moment in the company's transition to electric vehicles.

To foster a strong consumer community and enhance global brand awareness, the company organizes initiatives such as the Lotus Day events, celebrated on March 29 in both 2023 and 2024. These events not only celebrate driving passion and racing culture but also coincide with vehicle deliveries, further engaging the customer base.

Moreover, the company integrates customer satisfaction metrics into its performance management system, which is aimed at enhancing marketing efficiency and ensuring that customer feedback is utilized to refine marketing strategies. This comprehensive approach underscores the company's commitment to delivering exceptional customer experiences while solidifying its brand presence in the automotive market.

title: Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Operating Highlights for the First Nine Months of 2024**

In the first nine months of 2024, the Company achieved significant growth, delivering a total of 7,617 units, representing a remarkable 136% year-on-year (YoY) increase. Deliveries were well-distributed across various regions, with each contributing between 18% to 35% to the overall total, supported by an extensive network of over 200 stores globally. The European market stood out with an exceptional 372% YoY increase in deliveries, while the Rest of the World experienced a 110% growth. Notably, new deliveries commenced for the Emira model in South Africa and India, and the Eletre model in Japan, South Korea, and the Philippines. Additionally, the unveiling of the Eletre Carbon variant for North America is anticipated, with deliveries expected to start in the first half of 2025.

Sales figures for the period included 3,983 units of "Lifestyle SUV and Sedan," reflecting a 126% increase from 1,762 units in 2023, and 3,634 units of "Sportscars," which marked a 149% rise from 1,459 units in the previous year. Regionally, Europe accounted for 2,683 units (35% share) in 2024, a significant increase from 568 units (18% share) in 2023. China recorded 1,880 units (25% share), a slight decline from 1,993 units (62% share) in 2023. North America reported 1,665 units (22% share) with no prior data for comparison, while the Rest of the World achieved 1,389 units (18% share), up from 660 units (20% share) in 2023.

Financially, the Company reported revenue of 19 units for the first nine months of 2024, representing a substantial 217% YoY increase from 6 units in 2023. Revenue from Lotus increased to 8 units, reflecting a 100% rise, while revenue from other customers surged to 11 units, a remarkable 450% increase from 2 units in 2023. Furthermore, Lotus Robotics secured contracts valued at approximately $130 million for intelligent driving solutions with various automakers, significantly enhancing its technological footprint within the industry.

Overall, the first nine months of 2024 demonstrated the Company's robust growth trajectory, marked by increased deliveries, expanded market presence, and substantial revenue growth, positioning it favorably for future developments.
